metadata.description: A group of compute nodes within a cluster that share capabilities, labels, and scheduling constraints. Models the construct that cloud platforms call a node pool or machine set — a homogeneous slice of cluster capacity with specific hardware characteristics (GPU, architecture, memory tier). Placement policies query node pool capabilities to match workload requirements to available capacity.

lifecycle.decommission: Provider drains and removes the node pool. Workloads on the pool must be rescheduled or decommissioned first.

name.description: The node pool name within the cluster (e.g. 'gpu-a100', 'general-purpose', 'infra').

cluster_ref.description: Resource reference to a Compute.Cluster (common-elements §2.5, ADR-025). Authored by handle; deferred resolution supported (claim-before-define).

oneOf.description: Short form — the handle of an existing Compute.Cluster.

node_count.description: Target number of nodes in this pool.

capabilities.description: Queryable capability advertisements — placement policies select pools by capability.

models.description: GPU model names (e.g., A100, H100, MI300X).

labels.description: Labels applied to all nodes in this pool (Kubernetes label semantics).

taints.description: Taints applied to all nodes — workloads must tolerate these to be scheduled on the pool.

relationships.name: cluster
relationships.description: The cluster this node pool belongs to.
